description_long: This is an irregularly shaped stone fragment with a rough, jagged exterior and a partially flat face that appears to have remnants of faded paint or pigment, possibly from an old mural or wall. The stone's surface is predominantly a mix of beige, off-white, and light brown tones, with some reddish and yellowish patches visible on the flatter side. The texture is coarse and uneven, with visible inclusions of small pebbles and mineral grains embedded throughout. One side is notably flatter and smoother, suggesting it may have once been part of a larger, worked surface, while the other sides are more rugged and natural. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a minor obstacle or barrier in an urban environment, such as a piece of debris or a decorative element in landscaping.
description: An irregular, rough stone fragment with a partially flat, painted face and coarse, jagged sides. The surface shows faded pigments and embedded mineral grains.
description_view_0: The flat face of the stone is visible, showing faded red and yellow pigments and a smoother surface compared to other sides.
description_view_1: This side reveals a highly irregular, jagged texture with many protrusions and embedded pebbles, lacking any visible pigment.
description_view_2: A rugged, uneven surface with a mix of beige and gray tones, showing the stone's thickness and density.
description_view_3: Another jagged side, dominated by rough, off-white and beige textures, with no visible flat or painted areas.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or as debris on the ground
attribute_pattern: Faint, irregular patches of faded paint on one flat face; otherwise, natural mottling and inclusions throughout the stone
attribute_weathering: Rough and weathered, with visible erosion and faded pigment indicating prolonged exposure to the elements
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces but could blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements; may accumulate dirt or vegetation at the base
